title: ornithopus sativus subsp. sativus
intro: Ornithopus sativus subsp. sativus, also known as Lucerne or Alfalfa, is a perennial legume plant native to Europe and Asia, widely cultivated for its edible and medicinal properties. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues due to its rich content of vitamins, minerals, and antioxidants. The health benefits of Ornithopus sativus subsp. sativus include lowering cholesterol levels, improving digestion, and acting as an anti-inflammatory agent, which can help alleviate symptoms of arthritis and other inflammatory conditions. Herbal preparations of Lucerne include tea, capsules, and tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
